tossups.question: This artist wrote the script for Sergei Eisenstein’s now-lost film, The Storming of La Sarraz. A film by this artist was originally scored by Paul Hindemith, but that soundtrack is now lost due to the destruction of the work under the Nazi “degenerate art” campaign. That film, Ghosts Before Breakfast, was eventually re-scored by composers like Jean Hasse and Ian Gardner. (*) Later in life, this filmmaker created pieces like 8 x 8: A Chess Sonata in 8 movements and Dreams That Money Can Buy, which starred Max Ernst. This man animated the movement and dilation of squares in what he claimed to be the first abstract films in existence; those films include Rhythmus 21. For 10 points, name this German filmmaker and painter, known for his abstract films and presence in Dadaism and Neoplasticism.
